title: Afrosciadium dispersum
taxonomy: Afrosciadium dispersum is classified as a species within the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Afrosciadium, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Apiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Members of the Apiaceae family are primarily composed of plants with umbrella-shaped inflorescences. Afrosciadium dispersum is native to the region of Southern Africa.

intro: Afrosciadium dispersum, also known as "African celery," is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and fever, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Afrosciadium dispersum include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Afrosciadium dispersum include infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which are used to treat respiratory conditions, fever, and other health issues.
